items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment document is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Crane manufacturer name, model and serial number are marked on Load Rating Chart
Operator is certified and licensed
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
Rated capacity of crane is marked (Arabic & English)
Erection and dismantling procedures are based on manufacturer recommendations
A durable rating chart, which is legible, is visible to the operator at the controls
Main structure components are free of defects (bolts, pins, mast sections for cracks, bent structural members, etc.)
Remote control operation is available
Crane stops when remote control signal (for any crane motion) is lost
Access ladders to cab are provided (platform surfaces to be skid resistant)
Foot walks and ladders are provided where appropriate (foot walks to be minimum of 18" wide with slip resistant surface)
Controls used during the crane operating cycle are within reach of operator
Cab door is restrained from moving during crane operation or travel
Electrical equipment are protected from the environment and guarded
Electrical equipment can be mains disconnected at ground level (in case of electrically operated cranes)
Load trolley can brake in travel mode (automatically in case of power loss and drive rope breakage)
Afire extinguisher is provided in the cab (minimum rating is 10 BC)
Exhaust piping is guarded or insulated (where personnel could contact it)
Fuel filler pipes are located or protected so as to avoid spillage
guards are fitted to exposed moving parts and can support 90 kg without deformation
Clutch is in good condition and protected against weather and oil contaminations
Limiting devices are fully operational for trolley travel, boom luffing, upper hoist limit, crane travel, lifted load and radius
The load moment indicator (LMI) is installed and properly working (if kg or more)
Slewing (swing) motion of the crane is smooth for both start and stop
Slewing brakes are operational and can be set to hold the position without further operator action
Counterweight is made in accordance with crane manufacturer’s specifications
Counterweight is fixed in place and (for movable weights uncontrolled movement is not possible)
Welded members/Joints are free of defects (cracks, corrosion, etc.)
A metal receptacle is in place for storage of small hand tools and lubricating equipment (in cab or machinery housing)
Brakes are protected from weather and other industrial liquids
Proper house keeping in the cab

HOISTING UNIT Remaining rope on the drum (load hoist and/or boom hoist) in the 2.1.1 extreme low position is at least 2 full wraps The drum end of the rope is attached to the drum as per manufacturer 2.1.2 recommendations

BOOM AND JIB 2.2.1 Standing ropes are not fiber core or rotation resistant ropes 2.2.2 Swaged or poured sockets (pendant ropes) are in good condition 2.2.3 Welded members/Joints are free of defects (cracks, corrosion, etc.) 2.2.4 Boom is free of damage/deformation (cracks, corrosion, dents, etc.) Boom stops are in good working condition (fixed, telescopic, shock 2.2.5 absorbing or hydraulic) 2.2.6 Luffing boom brake can provide 125% or more of the full load torque Wind velocity measuring device (Anemometer) is rotating freely and 2.2.7 sending a visible or audible alarm signal to the display in the cab
MAST STRUCTURE 2.3.1 Travel rails are level and secure 2.3.2 Rails are grounded (for electrically powered cranes)

CABIN 2.4.1 Control levers and/or pedals functions are labeled 2.4.2 Operator cab has a safety glazing glass Cab lighting (artificial or natural) is sufficient to enable the operator to 2.4.3 observe the controls 2.4.4 Operator cab audible signal devices are operable and in good condition Emergency stop is effective (when there is a remote control device 2.4.5 malfunction) Simultaneous activation of controls is not possible when more than one 2.4.6 operator station (remote control) is provided
SLEWING COMPONENTS 2.5.1 Lubrication points are accessible

POWER PLANT AND ELECTRICALS 2.6.1 Engine speed control is working satisfactorily 2.6.2 Transmission selector can be operated satisfactorily 2.6.3 Engine emergency stop switch is working properly Pedal is slip resistance and a means is provided for holding the brakes in 2.6.4 the applied position Travel distance extremes of hand levers and pedals are acceptable 2.6.5 • 14”-24” for hand levers • 10” for pedals

HOOKS 2.7.1 Labeling and manufacturer data are available and legible 2.7.2 Safe working load is clearly marked on the hook 2.7.3 Hook is not bent or twisted Hook is not distorted in the throat opening 2.7.4 • Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1∕4 in. (6 mm) or as recommended by the manufacturer Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10% (compared to 2.7.5 new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations 2.7.6 Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks 2.7.7 Hook can lock (if it is a self-locking hook) Inoperative latch (if provided) — any damaged latch or malfunctioning 2.7.8 latch that does not close the hook’s throat
